title: echinochloa frumentacea var. atherachne
intro: Echinochloa frumentacea var. atherachne, also known as Japanese millet or shirohie, is a type of plant that belongs to the grass family (Poaceae). It has been traditionally used in Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Echinochloa frumentacea var. atherachne include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting digestive health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or decoctions, as well as being dried and used as a powder or tablet supplement.
